Date of hearing: 15.09.2021. Appellants by: Syed Zeeshan Haider, Advocate. State by: Ch. Asghar Ali Gill, Deputy Prosecutor General. Complainant by: Mr. Ahmed Sher Kathia, Advocate. SADIQ MAHMUD KHURRAM, J. –Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya and Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq (convicts) were tried along with Mohammad Maki son of Abdul Hameed, Mohammad Ishaq son of Ghulam Mustafa, Mohammad Bilal son of Ghulam Qadir, Mohammad Jameel son of Ghulam Mohammad, Mohammad Rafique son of Ghulam Ali, Ejaz Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. The learned trial court vide judgment dated 25.01.2017, convicted Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya and Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq (convicts) and sentenced them as infra: Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya: i) Death under section 302(b) PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Naveed Akhtar son of Muhammad Hashim (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.100,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of both the deceased namely Abdul Waheed son of Muhammad Hashim (deceased) and Naveed Akhtar son of Muhammad Hashim, in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. ii) Imprisonment for life under section 460 PPC Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq: Imprisonment for life on two counts under section 460 PPC and directed to pay Rs.100,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of both the deceased namely Abdul Waheed son of Muhammad Hashim (deceased) and Naveed Akhtar son of Muhammad Hashim, in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Feeling aggrieved, Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq lodged Criminal Appeal No.85 of 2017 assailing his conviction and sentence. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentence of death awarded to the appellant namely Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya. The complainant of the case namely Muhammad Hashim filed Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017 seeking permission to file an appeal against the acquittal of Mohammad Maki son of Abdul Hameed, Mohammad Ishaq son of Ghulam Mustafa,Mohammad Bilal son of Ghulam Qadir, Mohammad Jameel son of Ghulam Mohammad, Mohammad Rafique son of Ghulam Ali,,Ejaz Ahmad son of Saifal, Abdul Ghafoor son of Abdul Majeed, Sajjad Ahmad son of Allah Wasaya, Mumtaz son of Khaleel Ahmad, Abdur Razaq alias Saqi son of Khadim Hussain and Mohammad Akram son of Mohammad Yousaf ( the co- accused of the convicts, all since acquitted). Precisely, the necessary facts of the prosecution case, as stated by Muhammad Hashim (PW-1), the complainant of the case, are as under:- “On 12.09.2014, I alongwith my family members and my relatives. Noor Hassan and Zahoor Ahmad, was sleeping in my house. My daughter Tahira Mai was sick, for which emergency tube light was on in my house. At about 2.30 am, the accused persons Ishaq son of Ghulam Mustafa, armed with pistol, Irshad son of Allah Wasaya, armed with pistol, Makki son of Haji Hameed, armed with pistol, Ejaz son of sifal, armed with pistol, Bilal alias Pappi son of Ghulam Qadir, armed with pistol, Jameel son of Ghulam Mohammad armed with pistol, Rafique son of Ghulam Ali armed with pistol, Wazeer Ahmad son Kareem Bakhsh, armed with pistol, Ghafoor son of Majeed, armed with pistol, Sajjad son of Allah Wasaya, armed with pistol, Muzamil son of Sadiq, armed; with pistol and an unknown person also armed with pistol entered my house. after scaling over the wall. After hearing foot fall we all woke up. The PWs Noor Hassan and Zahoor Ahmad also woke up. In our sight the accused Ishaq, fired with pistol at front of right side of neck of my son Mohammad Naveed. The accused Irshad also made fire with pistol at Mohammad Naveed, which landed on left side of left side of (sic) front of neck. The accused Makki also made fire with pistol which landed on right side of front of chest of Mohammad Naveed, on right nipple. The accused Ejaz also made fire with pistol at Mohammad Naveed, which landed on left side of his collar bone (clavicle). The accused Jameel also made fire shot with pistol at right shoulder of Mohammad Naveed.

The accused Ejaz also made fire with pistol at Mohammad Naveed, which landed on left side of his collar bone (clavicle). The accused Jameel also made fire shot with pistol at right shoulder of Mohammad Naveed. The accused Rafique also fired with pistol which landed on back of right shoulder of Mohammad Naveed. The accused Bilal alias Pappi fired at my other son Abdul Waheed with pistol, which landed on back of left side of his head. The accused Ishaq also made fire with pistol which hit left palm of Abdul Waheed. The absconding accused Wazeer, Abdul Ghafoor, Sajjad, (Muzamil) and unknown person made indiscriminate fire and did not allow anyone of us to intervene and rescue the deceased Mohammad Naveed and Abdul Waheed, as we all were un-armed. We raised hue and cry upon which our neighbours attracted the spot while the accused persons fled away. My son Mohammad Naveed succumbed to the injuries at the Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Crl. Revision No. 25 of 2017 5 spot. Whereas my other son Abdul Waheed was taken to hospital by us in serious condition but he expired at B.V.Hospital, Bahawalpur at about 5.15 a.m as a result of the injuries received by him. The said accused persons committed the occurrence on instigation ( ایما) of the accused Mumtaz, Razaq and Mohammad Akram, as they supported the accused Mohammad Jameel. The motive behind the occurrence was that the accused Jameel suspected illicit relations of my son Abdul Waheed with his (Jameel's) daughter Rukhsana Bibi but it was not correct. I got submitted the application Ex.PA, on the basis of which FIR No.264/2014 against the said accused persons was registered. The accused were summoned to face trial in the case instituted upon the private complaint titled “Muhammad Hashim Vs. Ishaq and thirteen others” (relating to FIR No. 264 of 2014 dated 12.09.2014 registered at Police Station Noshehra Jadeed District Bahawalpur) in respect of offences under sections 302, 460, 452, 324,148, 149 and 109 PPC for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Abdul Waheed son of Muhammad Hashim (deceased) and Naveed Akhtar son of Muhammad Hashim (deceased). The learned trial court framed the charge against the accused on 27.04.2016, to which the accused pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. 5. The complainant of the case in order to prove its case got recorded statements of as many as three witnesses. The ocular account of the case was furnished by Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) and Zahoor Ahmad (PW-2). Muhammad Ajmal (PW-3) stated that one year after the occurrence the appellant namely Muhammad Muzammil got recovered the pistol (P-3) along Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Crl. Revision No. 25 of 2017 6 with two live bullets (P-4/1-2) which were taken into possession through recovery memo (Exh.PG). 6. The learned trial court also examined as many as twelve witnesses as court witnesses. Muhammad Hussain, ASI (CW-2) stated that on 12.09.2014 he recorded the formal F.I.R of the case (CW-2/1).

The learned trial court also examined as many as twelve witnesses as court witnesses. Muhammad Hussain, ASI (CW-2) stated that on 12.09.2014 he recorded the formal F.I.R of the case (CW-2/1). Muhammad Asif, ASI (CW-3) stated that on 28.11.2014 non-bailable warrants of arrest (Mark-A) of the appellant namely Muhammad Muzammil were handed over to him for execution for execution, however, the same could not be executed and subsequently a proclamation under section 87 of the Cr.P.C. (Mark-B) was issued which he was deputed to publish. Ghulam Mustafa 1331/C (CW-4) stated that on 12.09.2014 he escorted the dead body of the deceased namely Naveed Akhtar for its post mortem examination and also received the last worn clothes of the deceased from the Medical Officer. Ghulam Mustafa 1331/C (CW-4) further stated that on 25.10.2014 Muhammad Arshad, SI (CW-7) received the Call Data Record from Muhammad Aslam, SI, Incharge Data Cell, Bahawalpur. Muhammad Nadeem Akhtar, draftsman (CW-5) prepared the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (CW-5/1).. Muhammad Nawaz 1033/HC (CW-9) stated that on 12.09.2014, the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to him two sealed parcels said to contain blood stained earth relating to the deceased namely Abdul Waheed and Naveed Akhtar and another sealed parcel said to contain empties which on 15.09.2014 he handed over all the three parcels to Rasheed Ahmad 263/C (CW-11) for their onward transmission to the office of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. Muhammad Siddique 1021/HC (CW-10) stated that on 23.11.2014, the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to him a Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Rao Jamshed Ali (CW-8) investigated the case from 25.09.2015 till 05.11.2015, arrested the appellant namely Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq on 28.10.2015 and detailed the facts of the investigation as conducted by him in his statement before the learned trial court. The learned trial court also examined Dr. Zeeshan Ahmad (CW-1) who on 12.09.2014 was posted as Medical Officer at RHC Uch Sharif and on the same day medically examined Abdul Waheed son of Muhammad Hashim (then injured later deceased ). A lacerated wound 6.2 cm x 1.5 cm, bone exposed on the left parital region of head. Skull was fractured (sic) and brain matter coming out. Margins were inverted. 2. On the same day, Dr. Zeeshan Ahmad (CW-1) conducted the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased namely Abdul Waheed son of Muhammad Hashim after his death and noted the same injuries as mentioned by him at the time of the medical examination of Abdul Waheed son of Muhammad Hashim, and further opined as under:- “After thorough external and internal post mortem examination of the dead body was of the opinion that injury No.1 was cause of death which caused fracture of skull bone rupturing of brain (vital Organ) leading and to cardiac and respiratory depression due to damage to these centers in the brain and finally death. All the injuries were ante mortem and homicidal. All injuried (sic) were caused by fire-arm weapon. Ex.CW1/3 is attested copy of the post mortem report and Ex.CW1/4 is its pictorial diagrams, in resepct of the deceased Waheed.” On the same day, Dr. Zeeshan Ahmad (CW-1) also conducted the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased namely Naveed Akhtar son of Muhammad Hashim and opined as under:- “Description of Injuries. 1. A lacerated wound 1.2 cm x.8 cm x through and through on the right frontal side of neck. Margins were inverted. There was blackening around the wound. This was entry wound. Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Crl. Revision No. 25 of 2017 9 2. A lacerated wound 2 cm x 1.6 cm x through on the left frontal side of neck. Margins are everted. There was swelling around the neck. This was exit wound of injury No.1. 3. Margins were inverted. 3(b) A lacerated wound 1.4 cm x 1.4 cm a round to oval in shape. Margins were inverted, Blackening around the wound.5 cm away from injury No.3(a). 3(c) A lacerated wound 1.2 cm x 1.3 cm. Margins were inverted.4 cm away from injury No.3 (a). 4. There was blackening around the wound. Margins were inverted....................................... FINAL OPINION. After thorough internal and external post mortem examination of the dead body was of the opinion that injury No.4 and injury No.5 were the cause of death which caused perforation and rupture of right lung (vital organ) leading to massive internal bleeding in the right chest leading to hypovolemic shock and death. All injuries were ante mortem and homicidal. All the injuries were caused by fire-arm weapon. On 25.10.2016, the learned counsel for the complainant of the case, gave up witness namely Noor Hassan as being unnecessary. The reports of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PH) regarding the analysis of the pistols recovered from the appellants namely Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya and Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq and the empties collected from the place of occurrence and the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PJ) regarding the analysis of the blood stained earth. 9.

On 25.10.2016, the learned counsel for the complainant of the case, gave up witness namely Noor Hassan as being unnecessary. The reports of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PH) regarding the analysis of the pistols recovered from the appellants namely Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya and Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq and the empties collected from the place of occurrence and the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PJ) regarding the analysis of the blood stained earth. 9. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellants namely Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya and Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, each of them replied that they had been involved in the case falsely and were innocent. The appellants namely Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya and Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq opted not to get themselves examined under section 340 Cr.P.C and did not adduce any evidence in their defence. 10. On the conclusion of the trial, the learned Additional Sessions Judge, Ahmed Pur East convicted and sentenced the appellant as referred to above. 11. The primary contention of the learned counsel for the appellants is that the whole case is fabricated and false. The learned counsel for the appellants argued that the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Crl. Revision No. 25 of 2017 11 issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible and relevant evidence to prove the same. The learned counsel for the appellants further contended that the statements of Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) and Zahoor Ahmed (PW-2) were not worthy of any reliance. The learned counsel for the appellants also argued that the recoveries were full of procedural defects, of no legal worth and value and result of fake proceedings. The learned counsel for the appellants finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the appellants beyond the shadow of doubt. 12.

The learned counsel for the appellants also argued that the recoveries were full of procedural defects, of no legal worth and value and result of fake proceedings. The learned counsel for the appellants finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the appellants beyond the shadow of doubt. 12. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant/respondent No.2 contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond shadow of doubt by producing independent witnesses. They further argued that both the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellants. They further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) and Zahoor Ahmed (PW-2). They further argued that the recovery of the pistol (P-3) from the appellant namely Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq and the recovery of the pistol (P-1) from the appellant namely Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya also corroborated the ocular account. They further contended that there was no occasion for the prosecution witnesses, who were related to the deceased, to substitute the real offenders with the innocent in this case. Lastly, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General and the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant/respondent No.2 prayed for the rejection of appeals as lodged by the appellants namely Muhammad Irshad son of Allah Wasaya and Muhammad Muzammil son of Sadiq. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellants, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant and with their assistance perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial carefully. 14. We have noted that according to the prosecution witnesses the occurrence took place in the dead of night at about 2.30 a.m in the balmy 12th night of the month of September, however, admittedly no source of light, which could have enabled the witnesses to have rightly identify such a large number of accused and also allowing the witnesses to note the individual roles of each and every accused present at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence, was produced by the witnesses during the investigation of case or even before the learned trial court.

We have heard the learned counsel for the appellants, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant and with their assistance perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial carefully. 14. We have noted that according to the prosecution witnesses the occurrence took place in the dead of night at about 2.30 a.m in the balmy 12th night of the month of September, however, admittedly no source of light, which could have enabled the witnesses to have rightly identify such a large number of accused and also allowing the witnesses to note the individual roles of each and every accused present at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence, was produced by the witnesses during the investigation of case or even before the learned trial court. Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) claimed that he had witnessed the occurrence in the light of a rechargeable tube light which was lit at the place of occurrence for the reason that his daughter namely Tahira Mai was suffering from illness and was staying at the place of occurrence along with him. The said Tahira Mai admittedly never joined the investigation of the case nor she appeared before the learned trial court to prove her presence at the place of occurrence during the night of occurrence. Furthermore, Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) admitted during cross-examination that he did not produce any evidence to prove that the said Tahira Bibi was being treated for any disease and since when Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) during cross-examination admitted as under:- “At the time of the occurrence children of Mst:Tahira Bibi were also in my house. My daughters. Abida, Rabia and Sajida are married and they were not present in my house during the night of the occurrence whereas my other daughters Tahira, Asma and Asia were present in my house. My Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Crl. Revision No. 25 of 2017 13 wife Noor Khatoon was also present during the night of the occurrence in the house. It is correct that I did not cite or mention any of my daughters Tahira, Asma, Asia, my wife Noor Khatoon and children of Tahira Bibi as witness of the instant occurrence anywhere nor I produced them before the police. I did not provide any documentary proof to the Investigating Officer showing sickness of my daughter Tahira Bibi, during the course of investigation.” The above referred portion of the cross-examination of Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) clearly establishes that he failed to prove the reason for which according to him a rechargeable tube light was lit at the place of occurrence.

It is correct that I did not cite or mention any of my daughters Tahira, Asma, Asia, my wife Noor Khatoon and children of Tahira Bibi as witness of the instant occurrence anywhere nor I produced them before the police. I did not provide any documentary proof to the Investigating Officer showing sickness of my daughter Tahira Bibi, during the course of investigation.” The above referred portion of the cross-examination of Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) clearly establishes that he failed to prove the reason for which according to him a rechargeable tube light was lit at the place of occurrence. Furthermore, Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) also admitted during cross- examination that he never produced the said rechargeable tube light which was lit at the place of occurrence before the Investigating Officer of the case during the investigation of the case. Similarly, Zahoor Ahmad (PW-2) also admitted during cross-examination that neither Tahira Bibi joined the investigation of the case nor the tube-light which was lit at the place of occurrence was produced before the Investigating Officer of the case during the investigation of the case. Muhammad Hashim (PW-1) during cross- examination admitted as under:- “I did not produce the said emergency light to the police during the investigation.................................... The said emergency tube light is also not present in the court room today nor I demonstrated the said tube light before the court at the time of filing of the instant complaint.” Zahoor Ahmad (PW-2) during cross-examination admitted as under:- Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Crl. Revision No. 25 of 2017 14 “During site inspection the police did not record statement of Tahira Bibi nor statement of any other female or any children, present at the time of the occurrence. The emergency light. was not produced to the police by the complainant................................ The tube light as mentioned by me in my examination in chief is neither present in the court at this time nor the police secured the same during the course of investigation.” Muhammad Arshad, SI, (CW-7), the Investigating Officer of the case, also admitted during cross-examination as under:- “The complainant did not produce any proof regarding supply of electricity in his house at the time of the occurrence nor the complainant produced the emergency tube light to me.

The august Supreme Court of Pakistan has held in the case of “Asadullah v. Muhammad Ali” (PLD 1971 SC 541) that both corroborative and ocular evidence are to be read together and not in isolation. As regards abscondence, the august Supreme Court of Pakistan has held in the case “Rasool Muhammad v.

Muhammad Ali” (PLD 1971 SC 541) that both corroborative and ocular evidence are to be read together and not in isolation. As regards abscondence, the august Supreme Court of Pakistan has held in the case “Rasool Muhammad v. Asal Muhammad” (1995 SCMR 1373) that abscondence is only a suspicious circumstance. In the case of “Muhammad Sadiq v. Najeeb Ali” (1995 SCMR 1632) the august Supreme Court of Pakistan observed that abscondence itself has no value in the absence of any other evidence. It was also held in the case of “Muhammad Khan v. State” Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Crl. Revision No. 25 of 2017 42 (1999 SCMR 1220) that abscondence of the accused can never remedy the defects in the prosecution case. In the case of “Gul Khan v. State” (1999 SCMR 304) it was observed by the august Supreme Court of Pakistan that abscondence per se is not sufficient to prove the guilt but can be taken as a corroborative piece of evidence. In the cases of “Muhammad Arshad v. Qasim Ali” (1992 SCMR 814), “Pir Badshah v. State” (1985 SCMR 2070) and “Amir Gul v. State” (1981 SCMR 182) it was observed that conviction on abscondence alone cannot be sustained. In the present case, substantive piece of evidence in the shape of ocular account has been disbelieved, therefore, no conviction can be based on abscondence alone. Reliance is also placed on the cases of “Muhammad Farooq and another Vs. The State” (2006 SCMR 1707) and “Nizam Khan and 2 others Vs. the State” (1984 SCMR 1092) and Rohtas Khan vs. The State (2010 SCMR 566). 23. It is important to note that according to the established principle of the criminal administration of justice once an acquittal is recorded in favour of accused facing criminal charge he enjoys double presumption of innocence, therefore, the courts competent to interfere in the acquittal order should be slow in converting the same into Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017. Crl. Revision No. 25 of 2017 45 conviction, unless and until the said order is patently illegal, shocking, based on misreading and non-reading of the record or perverse. The said principle has been enunciated by the august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the judgment reported as Muhammad Inayat Versus The State ( 1998 SCMR 1854) wherein it has been held as under: “The Court would not interfere with acquittal merely because on re­appraisal of the evidence it comes to the conclusion different from that of the Court acquitting the accused provided both the conclusions are reasonably possible. If, however, the conclusion reached by that Court was such that no reasonable person would conceivably reach the same, and was impossible then this Court would interfere in exceptional cases on overwhelming proof resulting in conclusion and irresistible conclusion; and that too with a view only to avoid grave miscarriage of justice and for no other purpose.

The important test visualised in these cases, in this behalf was that the finding sought to be interfered with, after scrutiny under the foregoing searching light, should be found wholly as artificial, shocking and ridiculous." The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Mst. Sughran Begum and another Vs. Qaiser Pervaiz and others” (2015 SCMR 1142) has held as under: “On acquittal, an accused person earns twofold innocence particularly, in the case when there are concurrent findings to that effect by the trial Court and the Court of First Appeal (High Court), is the bedrock principle of justice. In a case of acquittal, the standard and principle of appreciation of evidence is entirely different from that in a case of conviction. Unless the concurrent findings of the two Courts below are found perverse, fanciful, arbitrary and are based on misreading and non-reading of material evidence causing miscarriage of justice, the Supreme Court would not lightly disturb the same Murder Reference No.13 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 51 of 2017 Criminal Appeal No. 85 of 2017 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.5 of 2017.
